zh.lifeSummary: 當Joanne Clay於1587年出生在英國艾可布萊崔時，她的父親Edward Clay-63歲，母親Mary Ann Carlton18歲。她與Robert Woodward在英國英格蘭艾可結婚。他們至少是3個兒子和1個女兒的父母。她去世於1640年11月19日在英國坎布利其爾卻斯特頓享年53歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Joanne Clay于1587年出生在英国艾可布萊崔时，她的父亲Edward Clay-63岁，母亲Mary Ann Carlton18岁。她与Robert Woodward在英国英格兰艾可结婚。他们至少是3个儿子和1个女儿的父母。她去世于1640年11月19日在英国坎布利其爾卻斯特頓享年53岁。

nameMeanings.name: Clay
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (Midlands and Yorkshire): from Old English clǣg ‘clay’, applied as a topographic name for someone who lived in an area of clay soil, a habitational name for someone who lived in a district known as (the) Clay, such as the one in east Notinghamshire, or as a metonymic occupational name for a worker in a clay pit (see Clayman ).

History: The relatively common English name Clay had several American forebears in the 18th century. Henry Clay, born in Hanover, VA, in 1777, secretary of state for President John Quincy Adams, was descended from English ancestors who came to VA shortly after the founding of Jamestown. The revolutionary war officer Joseph Clay, also a member of the Continental Congress, was a native of Yorkshire, England, who emigrated to GA in 1760 and was a founder of the University of Georgia.

variants.name: Klee
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Ernst, Hans, Hellmut, Helmut, Horst, Kurt, Manfred, Otmar, Otto, Wolfgang.
German: possibly a nickname for a clover farmer, from Klee ‘clover’. Compare Clay and Clee .

variants.name: Clayman
meanings: English: from Middle English cleymann, from cley ‘clay’ (see Clay ) + mann ‘man’, an occupational name for one who prepared clay for use in brick making or pottery, or for a plasterer (see Clare 3).
Americanized form of German and Jewish (Ashkenazic) Kleimann, or of Jewish Kleiman , Klayman ‘small man’.

nameMeanings.name: Joanne
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From Old French Jo(h)anne, and so a doublet of Joan . This too was revived as a given name in its own right in the first half of the 20th century. It has to some extent been influenced by the independently formed combination Jo Anne.

variants.name: Johanne
meanings: and Norwegian feminine form of   Johan .

variants.name: Joann
meanings: From Old French Jo(h)anne, and so a doublet of Joan . This too was revived as a given name in its own right in the first half of the 20th century. It has to some extent been influenced by the independently formed combination Jo Anne.

historicEvents.title: Elizabeth I Signs Death Warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots
historicEvents.description: Elizabeth I signed the death warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots, on February 1, 1587, as she was found guilty of plotting to assasinate Elizabeth. Mary, Queen of Scots, was beheaded at Fotheringhay Castle in England on February 8, 1587.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Elizabeth I.org - Mary Queen of Scots

historicEvents.title: The Honourable East India Company
historicEvents.description: The Honourable East India Company, a British joint-stock company, was established in 1600 for trade in the Indian Ocean region. At its height, the British East India Company had a private army which was twice the size of the British Army, ruled large sections of India, and revenues in the millions.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Historic UK - The East India Company

historicEvents.title: The Pilgrims Board the Mayflower
historicEvents.description: "The Pilgrims boarded the Mayflower in search of religious freedom in the New World in September 1620. The Mayflower set sail from Plymouth, England, with 102 passengers, 40 of whom called themselves ""Saints."" After two stormy months at sea, the ship reached the New World. Nearly half of the group died during their first winter due to malnutrition and the harsh New England winter."

en.lifeSummary: When Joanne Clay was born in 1587, in Braintree, Essex, England, her father, Edward Clay, was -63 and her mother, Mary Ann Carlton, was 18. She married Robert Woodward in Essex, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 3 sons and 1 daughter. She died on 19 November 1640, in Chesterton, Cambridgeshire, England, at the age of 53.

Elle est décédée le 19 novembre 1640, à Chesterton, Cambridgeshire, Angleterre, à l’âge de 53 ans.
